ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR JAN-05-2021 CONTINUED TO MAR-02-2021 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR FAILURE TO OBTAIN AN ANSWER(S) FROM OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AGAINST DEFENDANT(S) ROJEL CARRANZA AS TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXTEND THE FIVE YEAR STATUTE OF CCP SECTION 583.310 PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 583.34 IS DENIED. THE DELAY CAUSED BY COVID-19 EXTENDED THE TIME TO BRING THE CASE TO TRIAL BY 6 MONTHS. IT WAS NOT "IMPOSSIBLE, IMPRACTICABLE, OR FUTILE" TO BRING THIS CASE TO TRIAL IN SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT BEYOND THIS EXTENSION TIME. THIS CASE WAS AT ISSUE ON MAY 23, 2022 AND PLAINTIFFS DID NOT ACT DILIGENTLY IN BRINGING THIS CASE TO TRIAL THEREAFTER. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFFS ALSO FAILED TO ACT DILIGENTLY IN BRINGING THIS MOTION, WHICH IS SET ON IMPROPER NOTICE GIVEN THE METHOD OF SERVICE. HOWEVER, IN LIGHT OF OPPOSITION ON THE MERITS, THE RULING DENYING THE MOTION, IS ALSO MADE ON THE MERITS OF THE MOTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2023 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Extend The Five Year Statute Of Ccp Section 583.310 Pursuant To Ccp Section 583.34
Off Calendar Oct-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Stephen V. Harrington; Manning Gross + Massenburg LLP; 925-947-1300; appearing on behalf of defendant Albert Hotel, LLC.

No other appearances. Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXTEND THE FIVE YEAR STATUTE OF CCP SECTION 583.310 PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 583.34 is DENIED. The delay caused by COVID-19 extended the time to bring the case to trial by 6 months. It was not "impossible, impracticable, or futile" to bring this case to trial in San Francisco Superior Court beyond this extension time. This case was at issue on May 23, 2022 and Plaintiffs did not act diligently in bringing this case to trial thereafter. The court notes that Plaintiffs also failed to act diligently in bringing this motion, which is set on improper notice given the method of service. However, in light of opposition on the merits, the ruling denying the motion, is also made on the merits of the motion.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
